name: invest-01-memory-leak-hunt
initialPrompt: Our Node.js API server's heap keeps growing until the process OOMs and restarts every ~6 hours. I need you to plan how we find and fix this. Specifically: instrument the process to capture heap snapshots automatically at growth thresholds, identify the top retention paths, and produce a fix + regression test so it doesn't come back. Also figure out whether this is a genuine leak or just V8 not releasing memory it doesn't need to.

cannedAuthorResponse.title: Node.js memory leak hunt and fix

blocks.text: Identify and permanently fix the heap growth causing OOM restarts every ~6 hours
successCriteria: heap stable after 24 h soak test
no OOM restart in staging for 7 days

blocks.question: Which heap snapshot tool do we use — built-in Node.js inspector or a third-party profiler?

blocks.title: Capture baseline RSS and heap-used metrics in production
acceptance: time-series chart of heap-used exported to dashboards
OOM event timestamps correlated with heap spike

blocks.title: Instrument service to auto-capture heap snapshots at 80% heap threshold
acceptance: snapshot written to /tmp on threshold breach
snapshot uploaded to S3 within 60 s

blocks.title: Analyze top retention paths in captured snapshots

blocks.description: Heap snapshot capture adds significant CPU pause in production
blocks.mitigation: Capture snapshots only off peak hours; use async streaming snapshot API

blocks.question: Is the heap growth due to a genuine reference leak or V8 deferring GC because memory is available?

idChangedButCorresponding.note: Same intent (genuine leak vs V8 lazy GC) but id was renamed during revise. Not in expectedPreservedIds; AC-13 re-anchoring must map a comment on oq-v8-lazy-release to oq-gc-pressure.

decoy.note: Genuinely new block. Shares surface tokens 'retention' and 'heap' with task-analyze-retention, making it a trap for the re-anchoring fallback. A comment on task-analyze-retention must re-attach there, NOT here.

notes: Renumbering pressure: task-repro-local inserted near top shifting positional alignment; task-regression-test appended as genuinely new; oq-v8-lazy-release renamed to oq-gc-pressure. Naive positional scheme loses obj/dec alignment after the inserted repro task. expectedPreservedIds excludes the id-changed open-question and the two new tasks.
